chief_complaint: A 28-year-old woman presented with a non-palpable Nexplanon implant found within a segmental branch of the right lower lobe pulmonary artery.
history: The patient had a history of a subdermal contraceptive implant inserted several years prior. She was asymptomatic until the implant was incidentally discovered on imaging.
exam: Vital signs were within normal limits. Physical examination was unremarkable.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Imaging: CT pulmonary angiogram showing a non-palpable Nexplanon implant within a segmental branch of the right lower lobe pulmonary artery.
correct_diagnosis: Pulmonary embolism of subdermal contraceptive implant
differentials: Pulmonary embolism of subdermal contraceptive implant
correct_ranking: Pulmonary embolism of subdermal contraceptive implant
